learning_outcomes.description: Understand and explain the role of the management accountant and management accounting information in planning, control and decision making of organisations.

Understand, be able to evaluate and explain how management accounting information must adapt and respond to changes in organisational strategies, structure and operations over time, particularly in a changing business environment.
Demonstrate ability to prepare, analyse and critically evaluate information to assist with costing, pricing and performance evaluation decisions.
Explain and predict the behavioural implications of management accounting systems for costing, planning and control for the organisation.

assessments.task_description:
The In-Semester Examination will be held on a Saturday after the content of Lecture 4 and its corresponding tutorial have been delivered. There are three possible dates; please refer to the UQ Calendar for details.

This exam is an on-campus, paper-based, closed book examination invigilated by UQ-appointed invigilators. It will assess material from Lectures and Tutorials (Topics) 1–4 and will comprise multiple-choice, short-answer, and practical/problem-solving questions. Multiple-choice questions will be computer-marked, while short-answer and practical/problem-solving questions will be marked based on both workings and final answers.

You must bring your student ID to the examination. Results will be released via the course Blackboard site. Further details regarding the examination format will be provided on Blackboard.

Approved deferred In-Semester Examinations will also be held on a Saturday; please refer to the UQ Calendar for the relevant date.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description:
Cases help increase your learning of the topic areas concerned by requiring the application of course material to business organisations. 

Further details will accompany the case study when released.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
The Final Examination is an on-campus, paper-based, closed book examination invigilated by UQ-appointed invigilators.

The examination will assess material from Topics 5–11 and will comprise multiple-choice, short-answer, and practical/problem-solving questions.

Further details regarding the examination format will be provided on the course Blackboard site.
